title: change `language_detection_threshold` default value
body: the current value of `language_detection_threshold` means that `language_detection_segments` will be 1 regardless of the user set value, the new value will ensure that `language_detection_segments` is respected

step_plan.summary: Update WhisperModel.transcribe to use a numeric default (0.7) for language_detection_threshold and update its docstring so language_detection_segments is respected by default; this aligns behavior with detect_language_multi_segment and prevents an immediate break on the first segment.

steps.operation: Change default language_detection_threshold and update docstring
steps.reason: The current default language_detection_threshold is None, and the detection loop uses `if language_detection_threshold is None or language_probability > language_detection_threshold: break`, which causes the loop to always break on the first segment and thus ignores language_detection_segments. Setting a numeric default (0.7) matches detect_language_multi_segment and ensures the loop will continue scanning up to language_detection_segments unless a segment's probability exceeds the threshold.
steps.side_effects: Behavioral change for callers that relied on the old None default: language detection will now scan multiple segments by default (up to language_detection_segments) unless a segment exceeds 0.7, which may change detected language for some inputs and slightly increase CPU/audio processing in transcribe. API surface (function signature) remains compatible (parameter still present), but the default behavior changes so consumer code and tests that assumed the previous immediate-break behavior may need updates. Document string and any user-facing docs/examples should be adjusted. No changes to batched inference are required by this PR, but consider future enhancements to use multi-segment detection there. Run unit/integration tests to catch any downstream failures.
